risk_flags: GB Power at 134.55 EUR/MWh (95th percentile) signals sustained thermal tightness; any nuclear outage or wind collapse extends premium into June.
EU Storage at 36.99% (14 pp below seasonal) with only 2 days of weak inflow (+0.33% daily) — refill velocity insufficient for H2 2026 demand/export coverage if Hormuz restricts LNG.
Hormuz escalation could force 8–12 pp LNG flow loss; direct transmission into TTF and power forward curves via scarcity premium.
UK sanction relaxation on Russian crude may ease global oil, supporting fuel-switch away from gas but paradoxically tightening power if industrial demand recovers faster than renewables offset.

base.summary: Thermal scarcity regime holds; Hormuz volatility contained; storage refill pace stable. Power and Clean Spark remain elevated but stabilize week-on-week.

downside.summary: Hormuz de-escalation or ceasefire signal; crude eases on UK sanction relaxation. Fuel-switch away from gas into power. Mild weekend weather softens demand. Storage inflow accelerates.

upside.summary: Hormuz closure or credible NATO escalation Friday; LNG spot cargoes reroute. TTF risk premium spikes; power forward curve extends. Cold-front demand surge.

themes: Thermal scarcity and fuel-switch regime — GB and DE power historically elevated
Hormuz disruption tail-risk on LNG supply and EU energy security
Storage deficit 14 pp below seasonal — refill pace constrains summer flexibility
Macro stagflation narrative supports elevated baseload power and gas
top_drivers: GB and DE power at multi-year highs (95th and 80th percentiles respectively) on thermal scarcity — Clean Spark at 93rd percentile confirms gas-to-power dispatch regime in full effect.
Hormuz geopolitical tail-risk and EU growth forecast cut reinforce stagflation narrative; energy scarcity premium sustained on structural LNG uncertainty.
EU Storage 14 pp below seasonal average with weak inflow momentum (+0.33% daily) compounds summer flexibility risk if demand recovery outpaces renewable supply.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 95th percentile and Clean Spark at 93rd drive thermal premium; Hormuz crisis geopolitical tail-risk elevates LNG scarcity markup on TTF and forward power curve.

watchlist.why: NATO Friday meeting signals escalation risk; closure would force 15–25% markup on TTF front-month and sustain GB/DE power premium into summer.
At 36.99% with seasonal lag of 14 pp, weekly inflows must exceed 1.0 pp/week through June or H2 2026 baseload power risk emerges.
GB at 95th, DE at 80th percentile; widening spread signals transmission congestion or nuclear outage risk in France; monitor hourly marginal cost.
Fell 24.24% week-on-week to 42.27% (52nd percentile); next wind drought would spike thermal dispatch and extend Clean Spark duration.

watchlist.metric: UK sanction scope and crude arb
watchlist.why: Relaxation may ease Brent; feedback into fuel-switch economics and EU coal-to-gas margin — watch coal daily settlement vs TTF spread.

watchlist_dated.name: NATO Hormuz escalation meeting
watchlist_dated.why: Outcome could trigger LNG supply premium shift; monitor for explicit sanctions or corridor closure language.
